budget: Close the unjournalled-ValueError escape class at the selflearn compilation runtime's AST preflight (META-17 gate-6 finding F1, P2, pre-existing). _ast_preflight (selflearn/src/selflearn/compilation/runtime.py:36) calls ast.parse at line 45 catching only SyntaxError; ast.parse raises ValueError -- not SyntaxError -- on NUL-bearing source, so at the call site (runtime.py:277) that ValueError escapes run() raw and unjournalled, violating both the journalled-refusal evidence contract and the RuntimeCompError normalization contract. The path-resolution subclass of this escape class was closed in 5d2fb52 (control-character preflight plus (OSError, ValueError) on the read guards, journalled under executor.malformed-path); this source-NUL subclass at the AST preflight was explicitly left open. Only reachable via a tampered hash-matched executor file, since json.dumps escapes NUL in legitimate compiler output -- which is precisely the adversarial surface the tamper evidence record exists for. Required behaviour: a preflight failure on NUL-bearing or otherwise ValueError-raising source must journal an executor refusal via the established _journal_refusal mechanism under an honest kind consistent with the existing executor.* refusal kinds, then raise RuntimeCompError; no raw ValueError may escape run() from the preflight site. Bringing the preflight's existing SyntaxError and dunder/builtins refusal paths under the same journalled-refusal contract at that call site is in scope if the chosen guard shape covers them naturally; rewriting the preflight's detection logic is not. Add regression tests proving: NUL-bearing source with a matching hash journals a refusal and raises RuntimeCompError (not ValueError); legitimate executor sources still compile and run unchanged; and the refusal journal entry carries the entry_id and honest kind. Scope is exactly the reserved paths: selflearn/src/selflearn/compilation (runtime.py expected; sibling modules only if the guard genuinely requires a shared helper) and selflearn/tests/test_compilation_runtime.py. No workplan scripts, no discovery package, no prompt/evaluator/deployment wiring, no doctor.py (its NUL-path reporting gap is a separate recorded P3, out of scope).
stopCondition: Stop and fail closed on: any edit outside the two reserved paths; any weakening of the existing preflight refusals (dunder access, __builtins__ reference), the executor hash-tamper check, the restricted-globals sandbox, or any existing executor.* refusal journalling; any change that lets a preflight or sandbox failure skip journalling; any new dependency; a failing acceptance command; or an unresolved P0/P1 review finding.
evaluatorAuthority: The implementing seat may not self-approve. Pi/NeuralWatt GLM-5.2 reviews the frozen base..head diff read-only per AGENTS.md and returns P0-P3 findings with file:line evidence, preserved verbatim under .review-store/. Any P0/P1 requires a new commit and a fresh frozen-diff review; after any fix round, re-freeze the card to the head actually reviewed before submit. A distinct coordinator actor runs integrate and accept. This card grants no promotion, deployment, evaluator-write, or credential authority.
nextCheckpoint: A hash-matched executor file containing NUL bytes is refused with a journalled executor refusal and RuntimeCompError instead of an unjournalled raw ValueError; every legitimate executor source compiles and runs exactly as before; all pre-existing selflearn and root suites stay green; and the ValueError escape class at the AST preflight call site is closed with regression tests pinning it shut.
